violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as raw beef and chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as fresh produce) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Food items were rearranged during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment is to be cleaned, sanitized, and allowed to air dry prior to returning equipment to service. Ice was disposed during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Cooked shrimp and raw tuna was observed thawing in its original intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood items within ROP packaging shall be completely removed from their original packaging prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen into the food item. Food items were observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit during the time of inspection and voluntarily disposed of. Guidance provided. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Scoops were removed from containers during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2026
The drain for the 3-bay sink in the kitchen area is leaking and is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/05/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Lo-mien noodles were observed cooling in the walk-in cooler with plastic wrap covering the sheet pans. Food items shall be left uncovered to cool until an internal temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below is obtained. Food items were prepared within past 2 hours and plastic wrap was removed from food items during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

White tuna filet was observed in the sushi 2-door low boy with intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). ROP seafood shall be removed from packaging prior to thawing or cold holding storage. White tuna filet was observed at 33 degrees Fahrenheit during the time of inspection and was removed from its original packaging. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen prep units and sushi low boys rubber gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2025

The coving in the dishwashing area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Coving and other physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/13/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. *This violation did not exceed the original correction date of 01/19/24. New Violation. Correct By: 01/19/2024

The floors in both the walk-in coolers observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Floors shall be smooth, durable, non-absorbent, and easily cleanable. *This violation did not exceed the original correction date of 02/29/24. New Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
Inadequate lighting observed in the Amerikooler walk-in cooler. *This violation did not exceed the original correction date of 01/31/24. New Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023

An employee observed drinking out of an open container in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. -Staff removed the open beverage container from the food prep area during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023
Tofu juice observed dripping on sauce used for ready-to-eat foods. Staff voluntarily disposed of the sauce during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023

The Nam Chow station was observed held at (62) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff voluntarily disposed of all TCS foods in the unit during this inspection. -Service was contacted. Establishment shall not store TCS foods in the Nam Chow station until it is able to maintain an ambient temperature of 41 degrees F. or below. *Inspector's thermometers checked at 32 degrees F. during this inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/29/2023

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, observed not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/19/2024
The door to the Employee's toilet room is not self-closing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024

The floors in both walk-in coolers observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Floors shall be smooth, durable, non-absorbent, and easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/29/2024
Inadequate lighting observed in the Amerikooler walk-in cooler. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/31/2024
